description: Engine Supervisor Polish & Debug Pass — fix startup bugs (60s delay, double spawn, no model auto-load), complete missing implementation, remove dead code, polish for production.

userStories.title: Fix startup ordering — ensureStarted before listModels
userStories.description: As a student, I want the app to start the engine immediately on launch, not wait 60 seconds.
acceptanceCriteria: In App.svelte initInference(): call performStartup() BEFORE listModels()
listModels() must NOT be the first command that hits supervisor.get_client()
Engine starts within 2 seconds of app launch (supervisor logs Starting immediately, not after 60s)
listModels() still populates available models after engine is running
cargo check --workspace clean
svelte-check 0 errors
userStories.notes: File: apps/codehelper/src/App.svelte lines 714-718. Bug: listModels() at line 716 calls supervisor.get_client(60s) before ensureStarted(). Fix: swap the order so performStartup() runs first.

userStories.title: Pass runtime mode preference in Start command — eliminate double spawn
userStories.description: As a student, I want the engine to start on my preferred backend in ONE spawn.
acceptanceCriteria: performStartup() in App.svelte passes runtime_mode_preference in the ensureStarted request
Remove the separate setRuntimeMode() call after ensureStarted() in performStartup()
engine_ensure_started Tauri command extracts runtime_mode_preference and includes it in StartupConfig
StartupConfig carries runtime mode so supervisor applies it BEFORE spawning (sets SMOLPC_FORCE_EP)
Only ONE 'Supervisor: engine spawned with PID' message in logs during startup
No IncompleteMessage errors during startup
cargo check --workspace clean
svelte-check 0 errors
userStories.notes: Three-layer fix: (1) Frontend: App.svelte performStartup() passes preference, removes setRuntimeMode call. (2) Adapter: engine_client_adapter.rs extracts preference into StartupConfig. (3) Supervisor: handle_start applies mode before spawn. Currently performStartup calls ensureStarted(mode:auto) then setRuntimeMode(npu) — kills and respawns.

userStories.title: Fix model auto-load after engine restart
userStories.description: As a student, I want my previously loaded model restored when the engine restarts after a crash.
acceptanceCriteria: When load_model Tauri command succeeds, send SetDesiredModel command to supervisor with model ID
When unload_model Tauri command succeeds, send SetDesiredModel(None) to supervisor
After supervisor transitions to Running post-restart, it checks desired_model and calls client.load_model()
If first load attempt fails, retry once after 2 seconds
If retry fails, log error and stay Running with model_id: None
After successful restoration, broadcast updated Running state with model_id populated
cargo check --workspace clean
userStories.notes: Check supervisor.rs for existing desired_model handling. Key gap: load_model/unload_model in inference.rs must inform supervisor of desired model. Also verify post-Running restoration logic.

userStories.title: Add transition validation to supervisor state machine
userStories.description: As a developer, I want invalid state transitions caught and logged.
acceptanceCriteria: supervisor.rs transition() method calls can_transition_to() before applying
If invalid: log::error with current and attempted state, do NOT apply
If valid: apply and broadcast as before
Dead code warning on can_transition_to disappears
cargo check --workspace clean with zero warnings on can_transition_to
userStories.notes: supervisor.rs: find transition/state-setting method. mod.rs: can_transition_to() at line 56.

userStories.title: Fix readiness state on engine death in frontend
userStories.description: As a student, I want the UI to show engine state correctly when it crashes.
acceptanceCriteria: In updateLifecycleState(): crashed/failed sets readiness.state='failed' and readiness.error_message
starting/waiting_for_health sets readiness.state='starting'
idle sets readiness to sensible initial state
StartupLoadingScreen shows 'Starting engine...' for starting/waiting_for_health
Banner shows crash/failure message correctly
svelte-check 0 errors
userStories.notes: File: inference.svelte.ts updateLifecycleState(). Currently crashed/failed only set engineHealthy=false but do NOT update readiness.

userStories.title: Fix client cache race — broadcast client before state
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the client available when consumers see Running state.
acceptanceCriteria: In supervisor.rs: broadcast client via client_tx BEFORE broadcasting Running state via state_tx
Ensures handle cached_client is populated before consumers react to Running
get_client_if_ready() returns Some immediately after Running is observed
cargo check --workspace clean
userStories.notes: supervisor.rs: find where Running state is broadcast. Ensure client_tx.send() happens before state_tx.send_modify() for Running transitions.

userStories.title: Remove all dead code — zero compiler warnings
userStories.description: As a developer, I want zero compiler warnings and no dead code.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove startup_mode field from StartupConfig (never read)
Wire current_state() into engine_health_only command OR remove it
Remove subscribe() if unused OR document why it exists
Remove checkHealth() from inference.svelte.ts if unused
Remove any InferenceState or resolve_client references in comments
Remove unused imports in all engine/ files
cargo check --workspace produces ZERO warnings
cargo clippy --workspace clean
svelte-check 0 errors
userStories.notes: Current warnings: can_transition_to (fixed in US-004), startup_mode, current_state, subscribe. Also check for dead imports.

userStories.title: Run cargo fmt and prettier — fix all formatting
userStories.description: As a developer, I need consistent formatting across all files.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo fmt applied to entire src-tauri directory
cargo fmt -- --check returns no diff
prettier --write applied to changed .svelte and .ts files
prettier --check passes on changed files
Commit as style(engine): cargo fmt + prettier on supervisor code
userStories.notes: Run from apps/codehelper/src-tauri for Rust. Run from apps/codehelper for frontend.

userStories.title: Reduce log verbosity — suppress reqwest/hyper DEBUG
userStories.description: As a developer, I want readable logs without hundreds of HTTP pool messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Set RUST_LOG filter on Tauri app to suppress hyper_util and reqwest DEBUG
Use env_logger or tracing_subscriber filter: info default, warn for hyper_util/reqwest
Supervisor lifecycle logs remain visible at INFO
Startup produces ~20 lines INFO, not 500+ DEBUG pool messages
cargo check --workspace clean
userStories.notes: Check env_logger init in lib.rs. Add module-level filters for hyper_util=warn,reqwest=warn.

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md with supervisor architecture learnings
userStories.description: As a future developer or AI, I need conventions updated for the supervisor pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove obsolete learnings about resolve_client, InferenceState, connect_lock
Add: Engine lifecycle owned by EngineSupervisor — never spawn/kill engine from Tauri commands
Add: Use tauri::async_runtime::spawn not tokio::spawn in Builder::setup()
Add: Commands get engine client via supervisor.get_client(timeout) — watch channel, no Mutex
Add: Runtime mode preference must be in Start command — never apply post-spawn
Keep all non-engine learnings intact (OpenVINO, NPU, DLL, frontend, packaging)
userStories.notes: File: CLAUDE.md at repo root. Surgical update — only engine-related learnings.
